title: Scredington Lancaster Memorial
crew_summary: 9 killed (7 crew, 2 passengers) including pilot Plt Off. Max Keiran Cummings

external_narratives.summary: Detailed blog post about the Lancaster ED439 crash at Scredington. The aircraft dived out of cloud near Highgate Farm about 45 minutes after takeoff on 18 June 1943 and burnt on impact, killing all nine on board. The crew included an Australian pilot (PO Cummings RAAF), a Canadian rear gunner (FS Taylor RCAF), and two RAF Corporals believed to be wireless technicians.
key_details: Dived out of cloud 45 minutes after takeoff
Pilot PO Max Keiran Cummings was RAAF

external_narratives.summary: Australian War Memorial collection entry for Lancaster Mk I fragments from PO Max Keiran Cummings RAAF. Records the Australian connection to this loss and preserves physical fragments of the aircraft.
key_details: Physical aircraft fragments held by Australian War Memorial
Demonstrates Commonwealth dimension of the loss

external_narratives.summary: IWM War Memorials Register entry for 'The Crew of RAF Lancaster ED439 OL-N' memorial. National register record documenting the stone of remembrance at Scredington.
Virtual War Memorial Australia page for No. 83 Squadron (RAF) Pathfinder Force. Lists PO Cummings among Australian casualties serving with this elite Bomber Command pathfinder unit.
key_details: 83 Squadron was a Pathfinder Force unit

local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep
